stories.title: Stuttgart BOSS Open Semifinals — Fritz vs Bublik, Shelton Awaits
stories.angle: FOLLOW-UP — Quarterfinals wrapped Friday night. Defending champion Fritz (No. 2 seed) faces showman Bublik (No. 3 seed) in one semifinal. No. 1 seed Shelton, who beat Shimabukuro 4-6, 6-3, 6-4 in QF, is in the other half. Final is Sunday June 14. Fritz survived back-to-back three-setters (Landaluce Thursday, Bellucci Friday); Bublik beat Mpetshi Perricard in QF.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Stuttgart SF day: Fritz (defending champ) vs Bublik. Shelton (No. 1 seed) in the other half. Fritz survived back-to-back three-setters this week just to get here. Bublik lost zero sets reaching the final four. 🎾 #Stuttgart #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Stuttgart finals picture takes shape today. Fritz-Bublik could be the most entertaining match of the grass season so far. Bublik leads their career H2H but Fritz has the edge on grass. Defending champion vs showman. 🔥 #Stuttgart #TennisFanrecap

stories.title: Queen's Club WTA — Boulter Stuns No. 1 Seed Rybakina 7-5, 2-6, 6-4
stories.angle: NEW — British wildcard Katie Boulter (No. 73) defeats Elena Rybakina (world No. 2, No. 1 seed) 7-5, 2-6, 6-4 in 2h38m. Boulter's second career Top 5 win. Rybakina had already played a three-setter earlier in the day (beat defending champion Tatjana Maria 6-7, 7-5, 6-0). Vekic (lucky loser) also through after Czech doubleheader. SFs: Boulter vs Vekic; Jovic vs Raducanu/Rakhimova winner (QF moved to Saturday).

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: BOULTER. Wild card ranked No. 73 beats world No. 2 Rybakina 7-5, 2-6, 6-4 at Queen's Club. Her SECOND career Top 5 win. Rybakina had already played three sets earlier in the same day. Home crowd delivered. 🏆 #QueensClub #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Queen's Club WTA semifinals set: Boulter vs Vekic. Jovic waits for Raducanu or Rakhimova. A lucky loser, a wildcard upset specialist, and two of the tour's fastest risers. Wide open draw. #QueensClub #TennisFanrecap

stories.title: Libema Open QF — Majchrzak Upsets Top Seed FAA 6-4, 6-3
stories.angle: FOLLOW-UP — World No. 4 Felix Auger-Aliassime (top seed, career-high ranking) eliminated by Poland's Kamil Majchrzak 6-4, 6-3 in 69 minutes on June 12. Majchrzak won 85.1% of service points, never faced a break point threat himself. Second career Top-10 win for Majchrzak. Medvedev vs Cilic in the other QF. SFs on Sunday.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: FAA OUT. No. 1 seed Auger-Aliassime beaten 6-4, 6-3 in 69 minutes by Majchrzak. Poland's Kamil Majchrzak won 85% of service points, never faced a break point. Career-high ranking world No. 4 goes home early. #LibemaOpen #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Majchrzak over FAA in 's-Hertogenbosch is his second career Top-10 win. 69 minutes, 85% of service points won — that's a flawless grass court performance. Medvedev-Cilic in the other QF decides his SF opponent. #LibemaOpen #TennisFanrecap

stories.title: Wimbledon 2026 — Record £64.2M Prize Money (20% Increase)
stories.angle: NEW — Wimbledon announces record total prize money of £64.2 million, a 20% jump from £53.5 million in 2025. Biggest annual increase in the tournament's 149-year history. Singles champions each receive £3.6 million (up 20%). Qualifying fund up 25% to £6.2 million. Context: players lobbied for £71 million and staged press conference protests at Roland Garros.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Wimbledon 2026 prize money: £64.2 million. 20% jump from last year. Biggest single-year increase in the tournament's history. Singles champions get £3.6M each. Players wanted £71M — the fight isn't over. 🏆 #Wimbledon #TennisFanrecap

stories.title: ATP Rankings and Grass Season Picture — Sinner Leads, Alcaraz Out
stories.angle: NEW — Sinner holds No. 1 with 13,500 points vs Alcaraz's 9,960 (gap: 3,500+). Alcaraz confirmed out of Wimbledon (wrist injury, also missed Roland Garros). Sinner defending his 2025 Wimbledon title while skipping all grass warm-up events. Wimbledon draw June 26, Championships start June 29. 16 days to go.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Wimbledon 2026 picture: Sinner leads at No. 1 (13,500 pts), Alcaraz confirmed out (wrist injury). 16 days until the draw. Sinner defends without playing a single ranked grass event. The field has never had a clearer path to No. 1. #Wimbledon #TennisFanrecap
